title: MPC Foundations
summary: The execution model behind Arcium: how distributed computation, shared trust, and confidentiality guarantees combine to make private workloads usable in practice.

bodySections.title: Why MPC matters here
bodySections.body: Arcium depends on distributed computation to avoid placing full trust in one machine, one operator, or one organization. MPC is what turns privacy from a promise into an execution model with concrete trust boundaries.

bodySections.title: From concept to application
bodySections.body: This category connects abstract MPC language to the questions builders actually ask: what gets hidden, which parties coordinate the work, what guarantees users receive, and what tradeoffs appear compared with fully transparent execution.

bodySections.title: What readers should leave with
bodySections.body: After finishing this category, readers should understand the difference between confidential execution as a product capability and MPC as the mechanism that makes that capability believable.

description: The execution model and trust assumptions behind Arcium.
